Question: Verify that RC and L/R have the dimensions of time.

(a) If the current in the circuit of in Figure 14.12(b) increases to $$90 \text{%}$$ of its final value after 5.0 s, what is the inductive time constant? (b) If $$R = 20 \text{omega}$$ , what is the value of the self-inductance? (c) If the $$20 \text{-} \text{omega}$$ resistor is replaced with a $$100 \text{-} \text{omega}$$ resister, what is the time taken for the current to reach $$90 \text{%}$$ of its final value?

For the circuit of in Figure 14.12(b), show that when steady state is reached, the difference in the total energies produced by the battery and dissipated in the resistor is equal to the energy stored in the magnetic field of the coil.
